paper_title: Tree-ring Stable Isotope Analysis and Climate Sampling

methods.description: This method involves extracting and preparing tree-ring samples for stable isotope analysis to develop a subannual isotope chronology.

steps.description: We extracted two cores per tree from 36 trees using a 12 mm increment borer.
steps.searchable_summary: extract tree cores using increment borer

steps.description: We sanded the cores, measured the tree-ring width, crossdated the tree-ring series, and checked the dating results using standard dendrochronological methods.
We selected five crossdated cores from four mature trees without missing rings and developed a subannual isotope chronology from 1900-2014 for each core.
We systematically split each ring into multiple and sequential 200 µm thin sections using a rotary microtone.
steps.searchable_summary: section tree rings using rotary microtone

steps.description: We extracted α-cellulose and measured the δ 18 O of the α-cellulose.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and measure δ 18 O of α-cellulose

methods.method_name: Sampling Site and Climate Data Collection
methods.description: This method describes the collection of tree-ring samples and climate data to study the relationship between tree-ring δ 18 O and water availability.

steps.description: We collected tree-ring samples from Pinus massoniana near the Zhurong Peak.
steps.searchable_summary: collect tree-ring samples from specified location

steps.description: We used monthly meteorological data from the nearby Nanyue station from the China Meteorological Data Service Center.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch meteorological data from Nanyue station

steps.description: We calculated monthly VPD using monthly RH and mean temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate VPD from RH and temperature

steps.description: We used monthly self-calibrated Palmer drought severity index (scP-DSI) values derived from the Climatic Research Unit (CRU) 4.0 dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch scP-DSI values from CRU dataset
